ModMetaData.name: Better Trade Colors
ModMetaData.author: Kyle Givler
ModMetaData.description:
Better Trade Colors completely overhauls the visual clarity of the trading and caravan interfaces by color-coding items based on quality, durability, and condition. No more squinting or checking info cards to filter out junk—instantly parse everything at a glance.
Key Mechanics and Color Priorities: - Tainted Apparel (Dim Red/Brown): Instantly catch items stripped from corpses before accidentally buying or selling them. - Damaged Goods (Vibrant Magenta): Items under 50% durability are highlighted in sharp purple/magenta, alerting you to low hitpoint gear before making a bad trade. - Quality Spectrum: Beautiful, non-clashing colors that map directly to the classic RimWorld quality tiers: * Awful / Poor (Greyscale) * Normal (White) * Good (Soft Green) * Excellent (Yellow / Gold) * Masterwork (True Orange) * Legendary (Cyan / Light Blue)
Technical Focus: - Zero Bloat: Built entirely from scratch to avoid the heavy overhead of older UI color mods (looking at you, QualityColors). - Ultra High-Performance: Uses lightweight Harmony prefixes on the core UI drawing pipeline. Completely avoids messy Regex replacements, string manipulation, or memory-heavy text caching (looking at you again, QualityColors). - Conflict-Free: Safely modifies the rendering colors without altering underlying data structures, maintaining robust compatibility with major UI overhaul mods.
Plans: I wrote this mod because I was unhappy with the architectural mess of QualityColors. While this iteration doesn't do all of the same global features, it provides a strictly better, lightweight foundation for exactly what I need. - Future updates will include customizable colors via a mod menu. - Customizable HP percentages for damaged goods.
